sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Liquidity covenant waiver for Inotiv (NASDAQ: NOTV) covers April test dates
feed.summary: Inotiv, Inc. (NASDAQ: NOTV) has received a short-term waiver from its lenders for the minimum liquidity covenant under its Credit Agreement. This waiver specifically applies to the liquidity test dates of April 17, 2026, and April 24, 2026, and does not amend any other provisions of the Credit Agreement. The action suggests near-term support from lenders, despite potential underlying liquidity pressures.

feed.title: Guidewire Software (NYSE:GWRE) CEO Michael George Rosenbaum Sells 1,200 Shares
feed.summary: Guidewire Software CEO Michael George Rosenbaum sold 1,200 shares of the company's stock on April 20th for $139.75 per share, totaling $167,700. This sale was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan and reduced his ownership by 0.55% to 215,206 shares. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations, and authorized a $500 million share repurchase program, while analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $249.85.

feed.title: SCHV: Unconvincing Value ETF
feed.summary: The Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Value ETF (SCHV) holds over 500 stocks based on value characteristics like P/E and price-to-book ratios. Despite its diversification, the ETF is heavily weighted in financials and has consistently underperformed the S&P 500 since its inception, as well as other value funds in recent years. The author suggests that, like many passively managed value ETFs, SCHV suffers from methodological weaknesses.

feed.title: Two restaurants at Bellagio Las Vegas debut late-night menus
feed.summary: Two restaurants at Bellagio Las Vegas are launching late-night menus. Lago, an Italian restaurant, began serving an after-hours menu of antipasti, salads, pizzas, and desserts on Fridays and Saturdays from 10:30 p.m. to 3 a.m. Yellowtail Japanese Restaurant & Lounge is set to introduce its own late-night offerings on May 22.

feed.title: O'Reilly Auto Parts: Time To Lean In (NASDAQ:ORLY)
feed.summary: O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) is presented as a high-quality, defensive auto aftermarket leader currently trading 15% below its 2025 highs. The article forecasts easing SG&A pressure, leading to consistent earnings beats and multiple expansion, and projects Q1 same-store sales at 5.6% against a street estimate of 4%. A clear upside to a $107 price target is anticipated, with further gains expected as operating leverage and earnings consistency return.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Dollar General (DG) After A 30% One Year Rally?
feed.summary: Dollar General (DG) has rallied 30.8% over the past year, prompting investors to assess if it still offers value at around US$124 per share. Two valuation approaches, Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio, suggest the stock is currently undervalued, with the DCF model indicating a 28.5% discount and the P/E analysis showing it trading below its fair ratio. The article also presents bullish and bearish "Narrative" valuations for DG, ranging from US$116.63 to US$175.00, based on differing assumptions about future growth and profitability.

feed.title: Starbucks CEO, international Brady Brewer sells $58,800 in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Brady Brewer, Starbucks' CEO, International, sold 588 shares of company stock for $58,800 on April 17, 2026. The transaction was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. This sale comes as InvestingPro analysis suggests Starbucks appears overvalued, despite a history of 16 consecutive years of dividend increases.

feed.title: AMD Stock Surges To Record Highs As Analyst Sees 17% Upside— What Is Driving The Rally?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares reached record highs, driven by optimism over demand for AI chips and a raised price target from Stifel, which now sees a 17% upside. Bank of America also increased its target, estimating significant revenue boosts for AMD with increased AI capacity. Retail investors are showing extreme bullishness, contributing to the stock's 33% year-to-date gain.

feed.title: Moderna starts late-stage trial of bird flu vaccine in US, UK
feed.summary: Moderna has initiated a late-stage trial for its experimental bird flu vaccine, mRNA-1018, in the US and UK, making it the first mRNA pandemic bird flu vaccine to reach this stage. The trial involves 4,000 healthy adults and is supported by a $54 million investment from the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI). Moderna CEO Stephane Bancel emphasized the ongoing pandemic threat posed by bird flu and the company's commitment to preparedness.

feed.title: Moderna Announces Initiation of Phase 3 Study of Investigational mRNA Pandemic Influenza Vaccine Candidate
feed.summary: Moderna has initiated a Phase 3 study for its investigational mRNA-based H5 pandemic influenza vaccine candidate, mRNA-1018, with the first participants dosed in the U.S. and UK. This study, supported by CEPI, aims to evaluate the vaccine's safety and immunogenicity in healthy adults, strengthening global pandemic preparedness against H5 influenza. Moderna has also committed to allocating 20% of its H5 pandemic vaccine manufacturing capacity for timely supply to low- and middle-income countries if the vaccine is licensed.

feed.title: A Look at APA Corp (APA) After 4.5% Gain -- GF Value $25.63 vs P
feed.summary: APA Corp (APA) shares rose by 4.5% to $37.60, yet GuruFocus assesses the stock as significantly overvalued with a GF Value of $25.63. The company's GF Score of 67/100 indicates above-average performance, primarily due to strong profitability, but insider selling activity and low momentum raise concerns for investors.

feed.title: Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (HII) Shares Fall 3.6% -- GF Value Says Still Overvalued
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (HII) shares dropped 3.6% on April 21, 2026, contributing to larger declines over the week and month. GuruFocus's GF Value™ indicates the stock is 37.4% overvalued at $378.21 compared to an estimate of $275.27, despite a strong GF Score™ of 86/100 for overall performance. Insider selling of $7.4 million without any buying activity in the last three months reinforces concerns about its current valuation.

feed.title: Regeneron’s Lunsekimig Data Expand Immunology Story Beyond Dupixent Dependency
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NasdaqGS:REGN) announced positive Phase 2 results for its bispecific antibody, lunsekimig, in treating moderate to severe asthma and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps, meeting primary and key secondary endpoints. This data represents a significant advancement for Regeneron's immunology pipeline beyond its core product Dupixent, suggesting potential new revenue streams. While early-stage, these results strengthen the company's long-term immunology strategy by reducing dependency on existing franchises and offering a new asset in competitive markets.

feed.title: Tractor Supply to host Demo Days
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company will hold its annual "Demo Days" on April 25-26 in Alabama, including the Greenville store. The event allows customers to test drive zero-turn and riding mowers on an obstacle course, with staff available to explain features and financing options. Participants can also get promotional items and enjoy a festive atmosphere with refreshments.

feed.title: Capital One quarterly profit misses estimates as bad loan provisions rise
feed.summary: Capital One Financial missed Wall Street's first-quarter profit expectations, reporting $4.42 per share against an anticipated $4.55, primarily due to increased provisions for potential bad loans which rose to $4.07 billion. Shares of the company fell in extended trading, reflecting concerns over future credit risk and a decline in net interest margin. The consumer lender, which is a major player in credit cards and recently acquired Discover Financial Services, highlighted the impact of the macroeconomic environment on its lending operations.

feed.title: Arista Networks (ANET) CTO Duda sells 58K shares after 32K option exercise
feed.summary: Kenneth Duda, President and CTO of Arista Networks (ANET), exercised options to acquire 32,000 shares and subsequently sold 58,000 shares through open-market transactions on April 17, 2026. These sales, executed under Rule 10b5-1 trading plans, occurred at weighted average prices between approximately $160 and $165 per share. Following these transactions, Duda retains significant direct and indirect holdings in Arista Networks stock.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma CEO Laura Alber sells $3m in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Laura Alber, CEO of Williams-Sonoma Inc. (NASDAQ:WSM), sold 15,000 shares of company stock for $3,000,000 at $200 per share, executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Despite the sale, which reflects a 49% return over the past year, InvestingPro analysis suggests WSM may be overvalued. The company has recently received positive analyst ratings and implemented strategic initiatives including a new brand president and a partnership with Oakville Grocery.

feed.title: Lawmakers push for legislation to address rising catalytic converter thefts
authors: By Caleb Wethington and Jordan Gartner
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines confirmed that the pilots of Flight 507 had to take evasive action to avoid a collision with Southwest Flight 1152 at Nashville International Airport. While Flight 507 was performing a go-around due to gusty winds, air traffic control instructions inadvertently placed it in the path of the departing Flight 1152. Both flights responded to onboard alerts, and the FAA is investigating the incident.

feed.title: Southwest pilots use evasive action to avoid collision at airport, company says
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines confirmed that pilots of Flight 507 had to take evasive action to avoid another aircraft at Nashville International Airport. While attempting a go-around due to gusty winds, the flight was instructed by air traffic control into the path of a departing Southwest Flight 1152. Both flight crews responded to onboard alerts, and no collision occurred; the FAA is investigating the incident.

feed.title: Moderna begins late-stage bird flu shot trial in U.S. and UK
feed.summary: Moderna has initiated a Phase 3 study for its investigational H5 pandemic influenza mRNA vaccine candidate, mRNA-1018, in the U.S. and UK, aiming to enroll approximately 4,000 adults. This trial is supported by CEPI with up to $54.3 million in funding and is a crucial step in global pandemic preparedness efforts. If successful, positive data from this trial, alongside Moderna's seasonal flu candidate mRNA-1010, will support global regulatory submissions, with Moderna committing 20% of its H5 manufacturing capacity for low- and middle-income countries.

feed.title: Advisor moves: Osaic, Raymond James and LPL add advisor recruits
feed.summary: Osaic, Raymond James, and LPL Financial have successfully recruited new advisors, collectively adding nearly $1 billion in client assets. Osaic expanded its Empowered Independence channel by adding Pointes North Wealth Management, overseeing $500 million. Raymond James welcomed Jeffrey and Madison Gore with over $225 million in client assets, while LPL Financial brought back Courtney Walker, who manages approximately $180 million.

feed.title: XS335092945 Bond Price and Chart — FWB:XS335092945
feed.summary: This article provides details about the Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. 4.188% 22-APR-2036 bond (XS335092945) traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. It includes key terms such as outstanding amount, face value, coupon rate, maturity date, and information about the issuer, The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., and its business segments. Users can also access advanced bond data by signing up.

feed.title: Reading International (RDI) EVP exercises 8,768 RSUs, 4,160 shares withheld
feed.summary: Reading International's EVP and General Counsel, Sidney Craig Tompkins, exercised 8,768 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) which converted into Class A Non-Voting Common shares. Concurrently, 4,160 shares were withheld to cover tax obligations related to this transaction. Following these conversions, Tompkins directly holds 152,419 Class A Non-Voting Common shares, and his RSU balance has been reduced to zero.

feed.title: Reading International (RDI) CFO converts 8,768 RSUs into Class A shares
feed.summary: Reading International's (RDI) EVP, CFO & Treasurer, Gilbert Avanes, converted 8,768 restricted stock units (RSUs) into Class A Non-Voting Common Stock on April 18, 2026. This transaction increased his direct holdings to 134,386 shares. The RSUs were part of a larger grant made in 2022 under the company's 2020 Stock Incentive Plan.

feed.title: HP shareholders approve amended stock incentive plan and board slate at annual meeting
feed.summary: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) announced that its shareholders approved all company proposals at the 2026 annual meeting held on April 16th. Key approvals include the election of twelve directors, the ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as the independent accounting firm, and the adoption of the Fifth Amended and Restated HP Inc. 2004 Stock Incentive Plan, which increases shares available for issuance by 73.6 million. Shareholders also approved the compensation of named executive officers, while a proposal for an independent board chairman was not approved.

feed.title: [Form 4] READING INTERNATIONAL INC Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Robert F. Smerling, President of U.S. Cinemas for READING INTERNATIONAL INC (RDI), exercised 8,768 restricted stock units, converting them into Class A Non-Voting Common Stock on April 18, 2026. This transaction increased his direct holdings of Class A Non-Voting Common Stock to 109,945 shares. The units, part of a grant from April 18, 2022, became fully vested on April 18, 2026, and were converted at a price of $0.00 per share.

feed.title: Reading International (RDI) exec exercises 3,685 RSUs into Class A shares
feed.summary: Steven John Lucas, VP, Controller & CAO of Reading International (RDI), recently exercised 3,685 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), converting them into Class A Non-Voting Common Stock at $0.00 per share. Following this transaction, Lucas now directly holds 32,091 Class A Non-Voting Common shares. These RSUs are part of a larger grant from April 18, 2022, which includes both time-based and performance-based vesting conditions.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) CEO Antonio Neri Sells 150,000 Shares
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) CEO Antonio Neri sold 150,000 shares of company stock for approximately $3.975 million on April 17th, reducing his stake by 8.16%. The transaction, conducted under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 plan, leaves him with 1,687,329 shares valued at about $44.7 million. This news follows HPE's strong Q1 earnings report, beating expectations with $0.65 EPS and $9.30 billion in revenue, and solid FY2026 guidance, as the stock trades near its 52-week high.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) EVP Sells $475,517.97 in Stock
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) EVP Fidelma Russo sold 17,001 shares of the company's stock on April 21st at an average price of $27.97, totaling $475,517.97, as part of a pre-arranged trading plan. This follows a previous sale of 34,001 shares on March 25th for over $850,000. HPE shares traded up on the day of the reported sale, and the company recently reported strong quarterly earnings and increased its FY2026 EPS guidance.

feed.title: Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) exec sells 588 shares under 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Starbucks' CEO International, Brady Brewer, sold 588 shares of company common stock at $100.00 per share on April 17, 2026, totaling $58,800. This transaction was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on December 3, 2025. Following the sale, Brewer retains 83,787.502 shares of Starbucks common stock.

feed.title: Perimeter Announces Date for First Quarter 2026 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Perimeter Solutions (NYSE: PRM) announced it will release its financial results for the first quarter 2026 on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, before the market opens. The company will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET on the same day to discuss these results, led by CEO Haitham Khouri, CFO Kyle Sable, and Head of Investor Relations Seth Barker. The webcast will be accessible through Perimeter’s investor relations website and a replay will be available until June 6, 2026.

feed.title: Roivant Sciences (ROIV) director gets fully vested 670-share award
feed.summary: Roivant Sciences Ltd. director Melissa B. Epperly was granted 670 fully vested common shares as equity compensation under the company's Non-Employee Director Compensation Plan. This transaction, filed via Form 4, resulted in Epperly directly holding a total of 16,474 common shares. The shares were awarded at a price of $0.00, indicating a non-cash compensation.

feed.title: SC business owners sue Dave & Busters over gambling laws
feed.summary: A group of South Carolina business owners has filed a lawsuit against Dave & Busters, alleging that the arcade machines in their Greenville and Myrtle Beach locations violate state gambling laws. The lawsuit, filed by SC Citizens For Equal Enforcement of Gambling Laws, LLC, seeks to highlight what they claim is selective enforcement of gambling laws against local businesses and asks for refunds for customers who lost money playing arcade games. The group's counsel states their goal is to bring clarity and equality to the video game entertainment industry in South Carolina.

feed.title: Plexus Corp Stock Gains 91 Percent Over One Year
feed.summary: Plexus Corp. (NASDAQ:PLXS) was a top contributor to the Riverwater Sustainable Value Strategy in Q1 2026, with its stock gaining 91.71 percent over the past year. The electronics manufacturer reported strong fiscal Q2 results, driven by its Industrial and Healthcare segments, and is noted for leveraging AI for process improvement. Riverwater Partners expects a market shift towards fundamental valuation metrics, which should benefit high-quality small-cap companies like Plexus Corp.

feed.title: Roivant Sciences (ROIV) director gets share grant with tax netting
feed.summary: Roivant Sciences Ltd. director James C. Momtazee received a grant of 469 common shares under the company's Non-Employee Director Compensation Plan. To cover tax withholding obligations, 94 previously granted common shares were net-settled back to the company. Following these transactions, Momtazee directly holds 109,499 common shares, reflecting compensation and tax mechanics rather than open-market trading.

feed.title: Wex COO Joel Dearborn Jr sells $612,500 in company stock
feed.summary: WEX Inc.'s COO, Joel Dearborn Jr, sold 3,500 shares of company stock for $612,500 on April 16, 2026, as the stock approached its 52-week high. The sale was pre-planned, and Dearborn still retains a significant number of shares directly and indirectly. Despite the sale, InvestingPro analysis suggests WEX is undervalued, and the company recently surpassed Q4 2025 earnings expectations.

feed.title: SMCI Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Super Micro Computer, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding purchasers of Super Micro Computer, Inc. (NASDAQ: SMCI) securities between April 30, 2024, and March 19, 2026, of an upcoming May 26, 2026 deadline to serve as lead plaintiff in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Super Micro made false or misleading statements by failing to disclose that a significant portion of its server sales to China-based companies violated U.S. export control laws. Investors who suffered damages as a result are encouraged to contact the law firm to join the class action.

feed.title: An Illinois water utility uses schools, farms and solar to protect sources
feed.summary: Illinois American Water is celebrating Earth Day by highlighting its commitment to sustainable operations and water source protection. The company has invested in over 12,000 solar panels at two water treatment plants, which are projected to significantly offset greenhouse gas emissions. Additionally, Illinois American Water is engaging with communities, schools, and farmers to promote water conservation and responsible land management practices.

feed.title: Quetta Acquisition receives additional Nasdaq delisting notice over late filing
feed.summary: Quetta Acquisition Corporation (NASDAQ:QETA) has received another Nasdaq delisting notice for failing to file its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. This notice is in addition to a previous one regarding non-compliance with minimum Market Value of Listed Securities (MVLS) and total holder requirements. The company has requested a hearing and plans to file its overdue report as soon as practicable, though there's no guarantee of continued listing.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma (WSM) CEO Laura Alber sells 15,000 shares under 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma CEO Laura Alber sold 15,000 shares of common stock at $200.00 per share on April 17, 2026, as part of a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established in October 2025. Following the transaction, Alber directly holds 958,524 shares and indirectly holds 33,773 shares through the company's 401(k) plan. This sale is viewed as a routine liquidity event, given the small portion sold relative to her significant remaining holdings.

feed.title: American Express Hypercard Deal Deepens AI Role In Corporate Spending
feed.summary: American Express (NYSE:AXP) is acquiring Hypercard, an AI-focused expense management startup, to enhance AI-driven automation for commercial expense workflows within its corporate offerings. This acquisition aims to deepen American Express's role in the day-to-day finance operations for businesses, making it more competitive against other corporate card and software providers. The deal reinforces Amex's strategy of using technology and AI to boost engagement with commercial and premium customers, though integration risks and competitive pressures exist.

feed.title: RBB Bancorp (RBB) Earnings Rebound And 28.1% Margin Test Bearish Profitability Narrative
feed.summary: RBB Bancorp (RBB) reported a strong Q1 2026 with a rebound in earnings and a 28.1% net profit margin, challenging a five-year earnings decline trend. The company's recent performance shows a 19.8% earnings growth, improved credit quality, and better efficiency, attracting bullish investors. However, skeptics point to the longer-term earnings decline and an analyst price target below the current share price, urging caution.

feed.title: Legislation to ban pharmacies from owning PBMs heads to Gov. Lee's desk, CVS Health may close Tenn. locations
feed.summary: The Tennessee House passed a bill, the Fair RX Act, that would ban pharmacies from owning Pharmacy Benefits Managers (PBMs), sending it to Gov. Bill Lee's desk after it also passed in the Senate. CVS Health, which owns the PBM Caremark, has warned that if the bill becomes law, it will lead to the closure of all its Tennessee locations, including 25 MinuteClinic locations, and the loss of over 2,000 jobs, arguing the legislation will not lower drug costs and is solely designed to benefit independent pharmacies. Critics anticipate increased prescription costs and legal challenges, noting a similar ban in Arkansas was blocked by a federal court.

feed.title: BofA cuts Tractor Supply stock price target on soft pet spending
feed.summary: BofA Securities has lowered its price target for Tractor Supply Company (NASDAQ:TSCO) from $47.00 to $44.00, maintaining a Neutral rating, due to soft pet spending and a weaker-than-expected first quarter. The company's comparable sales of 0.5% primarily fell short because of reduced shopping frequency and a 100 basis point drag from companion animal sales, although inflation contributed positively. Despite these near-term challenges, InvestingPro data indicates the stock is undervalued and the company has consistently raised its dividend for 16 years.

feed.title: BMO raises Steel Dynamics stock price target to $240 on earnings outlook
feed.summary: BMO Capital has increased its price target for Steel Dynamics (NASDAQ:STLD) to $240 from $195, maintaining an Outperform rating, following the company's strong Q1 2026 results. The firm cited higher lagged sheet prices and improved aluminum mill profitability as key drivers for expected earnings growth. Steel Dynamics has also demonstrated a commitment to shareholder returns through consistent share buybacks and 13 consecutive years of dividend increases.

feed.title: Ex-patient grateful for funding as Jim Pattison Children's Hospital gets $2M from Mosaic
feed.summary: The Mosaic Company has donated $2 million to the Jim Pattison Children's Hospital Foundation, continuing its long-term partnership. This funding will support the hospital's Child Life program, which provides activities and support to help children cope with treatment, as well as the Child Life Zone. An 18-year-old cancer survivor, Abir Raiyan, expressed profound gratitude for the program's positive impact during his three years of treatment at the hospital.

feed.title: Irving-Based McKesson Sells $1.25B Stake in Medical-Surgical Unit Ahead of Planned IPO
feed.summary: McKesson Corporation has sold a minority stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions (MMS) business to Apollo Funds for $1.25 billion, valuing the unit at $13 billion. This transaction is a strategic step as McKesson prepares to spin off MMS into an independent, publicly traded company, with an IPO planned for the second half of 2027. McKesson will maintain operating control and majority ownership of MMS, emphasizing the move to unlock shareholder value and focus on high-growth opportunities within its core business.

feed.title: WR Berkley Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
feed.summary: WR Berkley (NYSE:WRB) reported strong Q1 2026 results with a record net investment income of $404 million and solid underwriting profits, achieving a return on beginning equity of 21.2%. Net income reached $515 million, or $1.31 per share, with a calendar year combined ratio of 90.7%. Despite increased competition, particularly in reinsurance and property markets, the company plans to focus on growth in attractive casualty lines while maintaining disciplined underwriting and expects to return significant capital to shareholders.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace Inc (HWM) Stock Down 3.1% but Still Overvalued -- GF Score: 84/100
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc (HWM) saw its shares drop by 3.1% to $247.72 on April 21, 2026. Despite the recent dip, the stock is considered significantly overvalued with its current price being 111.2% above its GF Value™ of $117.28. The company holds a strong GF Score™ of 84/100, driven by excellent growth and profitability, but its valuation score of 1/10 indicates significant pricing concerns.

feed.title: Elon Musk Agrees GTA 6 Could Be Made 'In Minutes' With AI — Take‑Two CEO Fires Back: 'Wouldn't It Take His Job?'
feed.summary: Billionaire Elon Musk suggested that AI could create a game like GTA 6 "in minutes," a claim that Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick countered by questioning its impact on Musk's own work. Zelnick argued that if AI could eliminate jobs, it would logically target the "richest man on Earth" first, highlighting Musk's busy schedule despite his use of AI. This exchange follows earlier remarks from Zelnick dismissing Musk's previous comments on AI's ability to create video game "hits."
